Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

Shouldn't we add also:

lustre/include/uapi/linux/lustre/ to the package?

I just tried to compile the TSM Copytool which rely on:
lustre/lustreapi.h, however since some older commits it breaks with:

fatal error: linux/lustre/lustre_user.h: No such file or directory

If I understand that correctly, then any
include "lustreapi.h" will rely on
linux/lustre/lustre_user.h which is not part of lustre-dev package.

Patch Set 1:

Thomas, there are a lot of .h files missing from the lustre-dev .deb package now, not just lustre_user.h.   The whole include/linux/lustre dir and all its contents aren't there,  This is a different problem than the one fixed in this patch, which only addresses the runtime environment and not the build environment,   Please file a new Jira ticket for that.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Ok, I will file a new ticket for including the proper header files in the dev-package.

Patch Set 1:

I will contact Ian King about what is the right thing to do.

commit.subject: LU-10558 build: put shared libs in lustre-utils .deb package
commit.message:
LU-10558 build: put shared libs in lustre-utils .deb package

Put the shared libs from Ubuntu build in the same package
as the tools that use them.

Test-Parameters: Trivial

Signed-off-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Change-Id: I0952efc8ead46cbf718e744087ab6d38016eacd7
